result.stdout:
0.0.1+70946b8ac7ed9496f807cd200c0d267687e7cfbe

InterBlaze CLI - A powerful tool for creating and managing Blazor Microfrontend applications

Usage: inter-blaze [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information.
  -v|--verbose    Enable verbose output
  --no-color      Disable colored output
  --no-telemetry  Disable anonymous usage data collection
  -?|-h|--help    Show help information.

Commands:
  add             Adds a microfrontend to an app shell
  analyze         Analyze InterBlaze project for dependencies and performance
  config          Manage InterBlaze configuration
  dev             Start development server for InterBlaze projects
  disable         Disables a microfrontend in an app shell
  doc             Generate documentation for your InterBlaze project
  enable          Enables a microfrontend in an app shell
  env             Manage environment configurations for your InterBlaze project
  list            Lists microfrontends in an app shell or available from a
                  repository
  migrate         Migrate and upgrade InterBlaze projects
  new             Creates a new app shell or microfrontend
  package         
  publish         Publishes an app shell or microfrontend as a NuGet package
  remove          Removes a microfrontend from an app shell
  scaffold        Scaffold components and features for your InterBlaze project
  template        Manage InterBlaze templates
  test            Run tests and diagnostics on your InterBlaze project

Run 'inter-blaze [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
0.0.1+70946b8ac7ed9496f807cd200c0d267687e7cfbe

InterBlaze CLI - A powerful tool for creating and managing Blazor Microfrontend applications

Usage: inter-blaze [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information.
  -v|--verbose    Enable verbose output
  --no-color      Disable colored output
  --no-telemetry  Disable anonymous usage data collection
  -?|-h|--help    Show help information.

Commands:
  add             Adds a microfrontend to an app shell
  analyze         Analyze InterBlaze project for dependencies and performance
  config          Manage InterBlaze configuration
  dev             Start development server for InterBlaze projects
  disable         Disables a microfrontend in an app shell
  doc             Generate documentation for your InterBlaze project
  enable          Enables a microfrontend in an app shell
  env             Manage environment configurations for your InterBlaze project
  list            Lists microfrontends in an app shell or available from a
                  repository
  migrate         Migrate and upgrade InterBlaze projects
  new             Creates a new app shell or microfrontend
  package         
  publish         Publishes an app shell or microfrontend as a NuGet package
  remove          Removes a microfrontend from an app shell
  scaffold        Scaffold components and features for your InterBlaze project
  template        Manage InterBlaze templates
  test            Run tests and diagnostics on your InterBlaze project

Run 'inter-blaze [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Adds a microfrontend to an app shell

Usage: inter-blaze add [options]

Options:
  -a|--app-shell      Path to the app shell directory
  -m|--microfrontend  Path to the microfrontend directory or package ID
  --route             Route path to mount the microfrontend on
  --id                ID to use for the microfrontend in the app shell
  --from-repo         Download the microfrontend from a package repository
  --repo              Repository URL to download the microfrontend from
  --version           Version of the microfrontend package to download
  -?|-h|--help        Show help information.

commands.payload:
Adds a microfrontend to an app shell

Usage: inter-blaze add [options]

Options:
  -a|--app-shell      Path to the app shell directory
  -m|--microfrontend  Path to the microfrontend directory or package ID
  --route             Route path to mount the microfrontend on
  --id                ID to use for the microfrontend in the app shell
  --from-repo         Download the microfrontend from a package repository
  --repo              Repository URL to download the microfrontend from
  --version           Version of the microfrontend package to download
  -?|-h|--help        Show help information.

result.stdout:
Analyze InterBlaze project for dependencies and performance

Usage: inter-blaze analyze [command] [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  bundle        Analyze bundle sizes and optimization opportunities
  dependencies  Visualize dependencies between microfrontends
  performance   Analyze runtime performance of the application

Run 'analyze [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Analyze InterBlaze project for dependencies and performance

Usage: inter-blaze analyze [command] [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  bundle        Analyze bundle sizes and optimization opportunities
  dependencies  Visualize dependencies between microfrontends
  performance   Analyze runtime performance of the application

Run 'analyze [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Analyze bundle sizes and optimization opportunities

Usage: inter-blaze analyze bundle [options]

Options:
  -p|--project          Path to the project
  -o|--output           Output file path for report
  --detailed            Include detailed breakdown by module
  --analyze-duplicates  Analyze for duplicate dependencies
  -?|-h|--help          Show help information.

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
Visualize dependencies between microfrontends

Usage: inter-blaze analyze dependencies [options]

Options:
  -p|--project      Path to the app shell project
  -f|--format       Output format (console, dot, svg, png)
                    Default value is: console.
  -o|--output       Output file path for non-console formats
  --include-shared  Include shared libraries in the analysis
  --detailed        Show detailed dependency information
  -?|-h|--help      Show help information.

commands.payload:

  --detailed        Show detailed dependency information
  -?|-h|--help      Show help information.

result.stdout:
Analyze runtime performance of the application

Usage: inter-blaze analyze performance [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the app shell project
  -u|--url      URL of the deployed application to analyze
  -o|--output   Output file path for report
  --threshold   Performance threshold in milliseconds
                Default value is: 300.
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Start development server for InterBlaze projects

Usage: inter-blaze dev [command] [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  proxy         Configure proxy for development
  server        Start development server
  watch         Watch for file changes

Run 'dev [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Configure proxy for development

Usage: inter-blaze dev proxy [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --port        Port to run the proxy on
                Default value is: 5050.
  --add         Add a proxy route in format 'path=target' (e.g.
                /api=http://localhost:5000/api)
  --remove      Remove a proxy route by path
  --list        List all proxy routes
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Configure proxy for development

Usage: inter-blaze dev proxy [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --port        Port to run the proxy on
                Default value is: 5050.
  --add         Add a proxy route in format 'path=target' (e.g.
                /api=http://localhost:5000/api)
  --remove      Remove a proxy route by path
  --list        List all proxy routes
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Start development server

Usage: inter-blaze dev server [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --port        Port to run the server on
                Default value is: 5000.
  --host        Host to bind the server to
                Default value is: localhost.
  --hot         Enable hot module replacement
  --https       Use HTTPS
  --open        Open browser after starting
  --env         Environment to use
                Default value is: development.
  --config      Path to custom configuration file
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Start development server

Usage: inter-blaze dev server [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --port        Port to run the server on
                Default value is: 5000.
  --host        Host to bind the server to
                Default value is: localhost.
  --hot         Enable hot module replacement
  --https       Use HTTPS
  --open        Open browser after starting
  --env         Environment to use
                Default value is: development.
  --config      Path to custom configuration file
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Watch for file changes

Usage: inter-blaze dev watch [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --exclude     Glob patterns to exclude from watching (comma separated)
                Default value is: node_modules,bin,obj,dist.
  --include     Glob patterns to include in watching (comma separated)
                Default value is:
                **/*.{cs,razor,cshtml,js,ts,jsx,tsx,css,scss,html}.
  --no-build    Don't trigger build on changes
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Generate documentation for your InterBlaze project

Usage: inter-blaze doc [command] [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  diagram       Generate architecture and flow diagrams
  export        Export documentation to various formats
  generate      Generate comprehensive documentation

Run 'doc [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Generate architecture and flow diagrams

Usage: inter-blaze doc diagram [options]

Options:
  -p|--project         Path to the app shell project
  -o|--output          Output directory for diagrams
  -t|--type            Diagram type (architecture, flow, component, all)
                       Default value is: all.
  -f|--format          Output format (svg, png, puml)
                       Default value is: svg.
  --show-dependencies  Show dependencies in the diagrams
  --detailed           Include detailed information in diagrams
  -?|-h|--help         Show help information.

commands.payload:

  --show-dependencies  Show dependencies in the diagrams
  --detailed           Include detailed information in diagrams
  -?|-h|--help         Show help information.

result.stdout:
Export documentation to various formats

Usage: inter-blaze doc export [options]

Options:
  -s|--source   Source documentation directory
  -o|--output   Output file path
  -f|--format   Output format (pdf, docx, epub)
                Default value is: pdf.
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Generate comprehensive documentation

Usage: inter-blaze doc generate [options]

Options:
  -p|--project            Path to the app shell project
  -o|--output             Output directory for documentation
  -f|--format             Output format (html, markdown, pdf)
                          Default value is: html.
  --include-api           Include API documentation
  --include-architecture  Include architecture diagrams
  --include-components    Include component documentation
  --theme                 Documentation theme (default, dark, light, custom)
                          Default value is: default.
  --logo                  Path to logo file to include in documentation
  -?|-h|--help            Show help information.

result.stdout:
Export environment configuration

Usage: inter-blaze env export [options] <environment>

Arguments:
  environment        Environment name to export

Options:
  -p|--project       Path to the project directory
  -o|--output        Output file path
  -f|--format        Output format (json, dotenv, xml)
                     Default value is: json.
  --compact          Export in compact format without comments
  --include-secrets  Include secrets in the exported configuration
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information.

result.stdout:
Configure environment settings

Usage: inter-blaze env set [options] <environment>

Arguments:
  environment      Environment name (development, staging, production, etc.)

Options:
  -p|--project     Path to the project directory
  --api-url        API URL for the environment
  --auth-provider  Authentication provider for the environment
  --cdn-url        CDN URL for the environment
  --log-level      Logging level for the environment
  --from           Copy settings from another environment as a starting point
  --vars           Additional variables in key=value format, separated by commas
  -?|-h|--help     Show help information.

result.stdout:
Convert existing Blazor applications to microfrontends

Usage: inter-blaze migrate convert [options]

Options:
  -p|--project   Path to the Blazor project to convert
  -o|--output    Output directory for the converted microfrontend
  -n|--name      Name for the new microfrontend
  --route        Base route for the microfrontend
  --interactive  Run in interactive mode with prompts
  -?|-h|--help   Show help information.

result.stdout:
Upgrade InterBlaze projects to newer versions

Usage: inter-blaze migrate upgrade [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  -v|--version  Target version to upgrade to
  --dry-run     Show what would be changed without making changes
  --force       Force upgrade even if there are warnings
  --backup      Create backup before upgrading
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Creates a new app shell

Usage: inter-blaze new app-shell [options]

Options:
  -t|--template  Template to use (EmptyServer, EmptyWebAssembly, EmptyMaui,
                 SampleServer, SampleWebAssembly, SampleMaui)
                 Default value is: EmptyServer.
  -n|--name      Name of the app shell
  -o|--output    Output directory
  --with-mf      Add sample microfrontends (blazor, blazormaui, react, python,
                 all)
  -?|-h|--help   Show help information.

commands.payload:
Creates a new app shell

Usage: inter-blaze new app-shell [options]

Options:
  -t|--template  Template to use (EmptyServer, EmptyWebAssembly, EmptyMaui,
                 SampleServer, SampleWebAssembly, SampleMaui)
                 Default value is: EmptyServer.
  -n|--name      Name of the app shell
  -o|--output    Output directory
  --with-mf      Add sample microfrontends (blazor, blazormaui, react, python,
                 all)
  -?|-h|--help   Show help information.

result.stdout:
Creates a new microfrontend

Usage: inter-blaze new microfrontend [options]

Options:
  -t|--template       Template to use (Blazor, BlazorMaui, React, Python)
                      Default value is: Blazor.
  -n|--name           Name of the microfrontend
  -o|--output         Output directory
  --language          For React: JavaScript or TypeScript
                      Default value is: JavaScript.
  --use-jsx           For React: Whether to use JSX/TSX syntax
  --css-framework     For React: CSS framework (Bootstrap, TailwindCSS, None)
                      Default value is: Bootstrap.
  --framework         For Python: Web framework (Flask, Django)
                      Default value is: Flask.
  --include-database  For Python: Whether to include database integration
  --database-type     For Python: Database type (SQLite, PostgreSQL)
                      Default value is: SQLite.
  -?|-h|--help        Show help information.

result.stdout:
Run tests for the project

Usage: inter-blaze test run [options]

Options:
  -p|--project  Path to the project
  --filter      Test filter expression
  --parallel    Run tests in parallel
  --no-build    Don't build before testing
  --blame       Enable blame mode to identify problematic tests
  --collect     Data collector for the test run
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.
